items.hook: The installation features a stainless steel sculpture with two 20-foot towers, designed by Kelly, and a 35-minute score composed by Stirling.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

items.hook: Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: King Edward I of England issues the Edict of Expulsion, banishing all Jews (numbering about 16,000) from England.
items.body: Edward I, also known as Edward Longshanks and the Hammer of the Scots, was King of England from 1272 to 1307. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Storms provoke severe flooding on the Saguenay River, beginning one of Quebec's costliest natural disasters ever.
items.body: The Saguenay flood was a series of flash floods on July 19 and 20, 1996 that hit the Saguenay–Lac-Saint-Jean region of Quebec, Canada. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Necrophiliac is the debut novel of French writer Gabrielle Wittkop (1920–2002), written in 1972.
items.body: A transgressive, epistolary novel in the form of a diary, it follows the life of Parisian necrophile, Lucien N. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: A Canadair CL-44 and Sukhoi Su-15 collide in mid-air near Yerevan, Armenia, killing four.
items.body: The Canadair CL-44 was a Canadian turboprop airliner and cargo aircraft based on the Bristol Britannia that was developed and produced by Canadair in the late 1950s and early 1960s. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: An Antonov An-22 of the Soviet Air Forces crashes into the Atlantic Ocean, killing all 23 aboard.
